stories.title: PHOTOS: Spain wins the World Cup by beating Argentina 1-0 in extra time
stories.summary: Spain won the 2026 FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time on July 19 in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal as Spain claimed their first World Cup title. The victory follows Spain’s strong performance in the tournament, with the team now favored for the 2027 Women’s World Cup alongside the U.S. Next, attention turns to the upcoming Women’s World Cup in 2027, where the U.S. and Spain are early betting favorites.

stories.title: Modeling agent who introduced women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
stories.summary: A French modeling agent linked to Jeffrey Epstein was found dead at his home near Paris on Monday. Daniel Siad, 69, had been under investigation for allegedly helping recruit women for Epstein over a decade. French prosecutors opened an inquiry into his death and ordered an autopsy. Investigators had not yet questioned Siad, who denied wrongdoing.

stories.title: Will Lionel Messi win the Ballon d'Or, and when will Argentina's World Cup star rejoin Inter Miami?
stories.summary: Argentina’s World Cup final loss on July 19, 2026 ended Lionel Messi’s 2026 Ballon d’Or hopes, as the evaluation period closes July 31. FIFA’s 21-day rest rule keeps him off Inter Miami’s next three matches, including games against Chicago, Montreal and Columbus. Messi’s return to club play hinges on Leagues Cup action, with Inter Miami opening August 5 against Atletico de San Luis. The tournament runs through August 12 at home in Miami.

stories.title: The video of the Argentina national team in the tunnel that buries the conspiracy theories about the defeat against Spain
stories.summary: A newly released tunnel video from Argentina’s World Cup campaign debunks conspiracy theories about their 2026 final defeat to Spain. The three-minute footage shows Messi rallying teammates before the match at MetLife Stadium, emphasizing focus over external noise. The match drew 63 million U.S. viewers, the most for any soccer game in the country’s history. Argentina’s next club commitments begin after a mandatory 21-day World Cup rest period.

stories.title: Four teens to appear in court after ‘serious’ Virginia incident
stories.summary: Four juveniles will appear in Cavan District Children's Court Thursday at 10:30 a.m. charged with a serious public order incident in Virginia, Co Cavan, Wednesday evening. Three people suffered significant but non-life-threatening injuries, and local waterways were searched after allegations of a weapon. Gardaí confirmed the arrests following an investigation into the Main Street disturbance. Further details are pending as inquiries continue.

stories.title: Garnacho becomes third Man Utd flop to join Aston Villa in three years as he completes medical for Chelsea loan
stories.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on a season-long loan from Chelsea with a conditional obligation to buy. The 22-year-old Argentine winger completed his medical and is finalizing the deal in Birmingham. The move follows a disappointing first season at Chelsea, where he scored eight goals in 43 appearances. Villa manager Unai Emery has agreed to a four-year contract if the permanent transfer is triggered next summer.

stories.title: ResilExec Coaching Launches Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™ -- Completing a Full Career Architecture Platform for Mid-Career Leaders in Healthcare and Enterprise IT
stories.summary: ResilExec Coaching launched two new career programs—Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™—expanding its platform for mid-career leaders in healthcare and enterprise IT. The additions complement the existing Purpose-Driven Ascent™ program, addressing external job transitions and post-promotion career stability. Founder Brian Baldari’s 8-phase system targets Associate Directors through VPs navigating layoffs or voluntary moves. The full platform now covers three key career moments: internal promotion, external re-entry, and long-term trajectory maintenance.

stories.title: Jasper Philipsen wins stage 17 of Tour de France, Tadej Pogačar protects lead
stories.summary: Belgian Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the 2026 Tour de France in Voiron on July 22, securing his 11th career stage victory. Defending champion Tadej Pogačar maintained his overall lead with a 4-minute, 32-second advantage. Philipsen’s victory extends Belgium’s streak of stage wins to three in a row. The race resumes Thursday with a mountainous stage from Voiron to Orcieres-Merlette.

stories.title: Was Daniel Siad ever married? Details on family amid key Epstein model scout's mystery death in Paris | Trending
stories.summary: French modeling scout Daniel Siad, linked to Jeffrey Epstein’s network, was found dead at his Paris-area home on Monday. The 69-year-old’s death prompted an investigation and autopsy, though authorities have not cited foul play. Siad’s name appeared hundreds of times in recently released Epstein probe files. Authorities continue to review his connections to the case.

stories.title: Kyle Kelly and Omari Patrick: Forest Green Rovers sign duo
stories.summary: Forest Green Rovers have signed midfielder Kyle Kelly on a six-month loan from Liverpool and forward Omari Patrick on a permanent deal. Kelly, 20, has eight caps for St Kitts and Nevis, while Patrick, 29, joins after scoring seven goals in 41 games for Tranmere last season. The club confirmed both signings ahead of the new campaign. Forest Green aim to strengthen their squad for the upcoming League Two fixtures.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 - Schedule, draw and prize money
stories.summary: The 2026 World Matchplay Darts tournament begins on Saturday, July 18 at Winter Gardens in Blackpool, featuring 32 players, including defending champion Luke Littler, the teenage world number one. The event is one of the Professional Darts Corporation's eight major events. The tournament will conclude on Sunday, July 26 with a women's event. The top 16-ranked players have been seeded to compete in the open draw.

stories.title: Braunau in Österreich: Hitlers Geburtshaus wird zu Polizeistation
stories.summary: Hitler's birthplace in Braunau, Austria, is being converted into a police station, a move aimed at neutralizing the site and deterring far-right groups. The building, previously a school and home to a disability support organization, has been vacant since 2017. The police station is expected to serve as a deterrent and help end discussions over the site's future use. The move comes as the city hopes to prevent the building from becoming a pilgrimage site for extremist groups.

stories.title: Gearóid Hegarty enjoying special days alongside his best friends
stories.summary: Limerick's Gearóid Hegarty and his teammates are celebrating their sixth All-Ireland senior hurling title after defeating Galway in the final. Hegarty's bond with his teammates, including best friends David Reidy and Seán Finn, has been key to their success. The group will soon disperse, with Hegarty set to get married to Niamh McCarthy in Portugal, and many of the players will not reconvene until mid-winter. Hegarty's selfless play, such as laying off a puckout to Reidy for a point, has been a hallmark of their championship run.

stories.title: USA: New Jersey registrierte versehentlich mehr als 6500 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler
stories.summary: In New Jersey wurden vor der Präsidentschaftswahl 2024 etwa 6600 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler registriert, aufgrund eines ernsten Softwarefehlers. Weniger als 400 von ihnen haben tatsächlich gewählt. Die demokratische Gouverneurin Mikie Sherrill hat sich über die verantwortungslosen Versäumnisse und den Mangel an Transparenz geäußert, die diese Registrierungen ermöglicht haben. Die Registrierungen fanden zwischen Juni 2023 und Juni 2024 statt.

stories.title: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam Prediction, Betting Tips, Lineups & Odds | 22 Jul 2026
stories.summary: Bodo/Glimt hosts HamKam at Aspmyra Stadion on July 22, with the home team seeking to extend their unbeaten run in the Eliteserien to seven matches. Bodo/Glimt has scored 31 goals this season, the most in the league, while HamKam has lost its last three games. The two teams have a history of close matches, with Bodo/Glimt winning their last meeting 3-1. Bodo/Glimt is looking to maintain its strong home form, having lost only one game at home this season.

stories.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
stories.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a man hiding behind a wall in a Wexford holiday home they had booked through Booking.com. The incident occurred last weekend when Therese and her family returned from a family party to find the landlord hiding behind a wall. The property has been suspended by Booking.com while an investigation is ongoing. The company has launched an investigation into the incident.

stories.title: Paul Nardi, un renfort d’expérience pour l’AJA
stories.summary: Paul Nardi, 32 ans, rejoint l’AJ Auxerre pour deux saisons jusqu’en juin 2028. Le gardien, ancien international Espoirs français, arrive en provenance des Queens Park Rangers où il a joué 63 matchs. Formé à l’AS Nancy-Lorraine, il a évolué en Ligue 1, Ligue 2 et à l’étranger, remportant notamment la Ligue 2 avec le FC Lorient en 2019. Il portera le numéro 1 et apportera son expérience à l’équipe pour la saison à venir.

stories.title: Text of new Gordie Howe bridge deal seems to contradict Carney
stories.summary: The Gordie Howe International Bridge near Windsor, Ontario, is set to open to traffic on July 27, despite recent trade tensions between Canada and the US. The bridge's opening ceremony, initially planned as a joint event, has been cancelled due to US President Donald Trump's threat to raise tariffs on Canadian imports. The deal to open the bridge includes a proposed agreement in principle, where Canada will split half of the bridge's net revenue with an economic development fund for the first 15 years. The bridge's opening will proceed as planned, with a Canada-only event on July 24.

stories.title: World Cup 2030 (very early) power rankings: who will challenge Spain at top?
stories.summary: Spain will enter the 2030 World Cup as favorites to defend their title, having won the 2026 tournament. The 2030 World Cup will be co-hosted by Spain, Portugal, and Morocco, with the first three matches set to take place in Uruguay, Argentina, and Paraguay. A total of 48 teams will participate in the tournament, up from 32 in previous years. The host nations are still deciding on the venue for the final match.

stories.title: Manuel Barragán vuelve a actuar después de casi perder la vida: "No hay nada que hacer"
stories.summary: Spanish comedian Manuel Barragán has made a remarkable recovery after nearly losing his life in a car accident, spending 40 days in the hospital, 17 of them in intensive care. The accident was caused by a heart condition that weakened his heart and led to a severe infarction. Barragán's family says his recovery was a miracle, and he has since returned to the hospital to thank the medical staff who saved his life.

stories.title: Wrexham to appoint Newcastle's Nickson as sporting director
stories.summary: Wrexham is set to appoint Steve Nickson as its first-ever sporting director on September 2, bringing 15 years of experience from Newcastle United. Nickson will work closely with manager Phil Parkinson to bolster the club's hopes of reaching the Premier League. This move follows Wrexham's highest league finish of seventh in the Championship and represents a significant shift in the club's transfer market operations. The appointment is seen as a coup, with West Ham United and Birmingham City also interested in securing Nickson's services.

stories.title: En direct Football. Le quatrième but du FC Metz face au FC Sochaux-Montbéliard : suivez le sixième match de préparation des Grenats en direct
stories.summary: FC Metz defeated FC Sochaux-Montbéliard 4-1 in a pre-season friendly on July 22, 2026, at Biesheim Stadium in Haut-Rhin. Pape Moussa Fall scored a hat-trick, with Jessy Deminguet and Mathis Clairicia adding the other goals. Metz dominated early, leading 3-0 by halftime before Sochaux pulled one back late. The result caps a strong start to Metz’s summer preparation ahead of the 2026-27 Ligue 2 campaign.

stories.title: Far Away, le anticipazioni delle puntate dal 20 al 24 luglio
stories.summary: La serie tv turca "Far Away" continua con nuove puntate dal 20 al 24 luglio. La storia segue le vicende di Alya e Cihan, che si avvicinano nonostante le opposizioni di Mine. La puntata del 20 luglio vedrà Alya e Cihan chiacchierare al bar dell'ospedale, mentre la puntata del 22 luglio sarà segnata dalla fuga di Kaya e Zerrin. La tensione tra le due famiglie sale in tutta fretta e Cihan cercherà di rintracciare Kaya prima che ci riescano i Nalhanoglu. Le puntate saranno in onda su Canale 5 e in streaming su Mediaset Infinity.

stories.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
stories.summary: Spanish influencer Lucía Pombo and pilot Álvaro López-Huertas have welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, born on July 21. The couple, who met as children through their parents' friendship, had been expecting the arrival of their baby for several weeks. With López-Huertas by her side, Pombo has expressed her excitement and relief at finally giving birth after a long and challenging pregnancy.

stories.title: Bohemians seek inspiration on the last European night at the old Dalymount Park
stories.summary: Bohemians host Ballkani at Dalymount Park in a Uefa Conference League qualifying round two first-leg match, with the Irish side looking to build on their recent consistency and home advantage after a 1-0 lead was taken to Tallaght in the first round. Bohemians are second in the League of Ireland, seven points behind Shamrock Rovers, and will be seeking a strong performance from their lone striker, Colm Whelan, who has scored nine goals this season. The match takes place on Thursday at 6pm. A win for Bohemians would set them up well for the return game in Kosovo next week.

stories.title: Hospital apologises after man's death in Galway
stories.summary: A 26-year-old Portuguese man, Henrique Coelho, died two days after being discharged from Galway University Hospital's emergency department in January 2024. The hospital has apologised for deficiencies in his care, which led to his death. His family has been awarded €35,000 in damages, with his mother receiving €20,000 and his ex-wife €15,000. The family had claimed negligence and breach of duty by the hospital.

stories.title: Asyl-Beratungsmobil fährt seit Februar durch den Landkreis
stories.summary: A mobile counseling unit has been operating in the Weilheim-Schongau district since February, providing on-site assistance to asylum seekers. The unit, which is a converted van, has already counseled around 70 people in 14 sessions. The unit is staffed by integration and refugee advisors from the district office, whose positions are also funded by the state of Bavaria. The unit's deployment plan is available on the district office's website.

stories.title: 10 clubs paid the most by FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Manchester City is set to receive an estimated $4.4 million from FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup, the largest payout among 10 clubs. The English champions topped the list compiled by The Athletic, ahead of Barcelona and Bayern Munich. FIFA will distribute $355 million to clubs that released players for the tournament, a 70% increase from previous tournaments. The payments will be based on the number of days players spent with their national teams.

stories.title: Tottenham vs. MK Dons: how to watch preseason friendly
stories.summary: Tottenham Hotspur is hosting MK Dons in a preseason friendly match at the Hotspur Way training center, marking the club's return to action after the World Cup. The match is a closed-door friendly, focusing on fitness and learning tactics rather than winning. The game is available to watch exclusively on SpursPLAY, Tottenham's in-house subscription streaming package. The match is part of Tottenham's preparations for the upcoming season.

stories.title: Avs, Ilaria Salis condannata per aver licenziato due collaboratori senza giusta causa
stories.summary: Ilaria Salis, eurodeputata di Avs, è stata condannata a risarcire oltre 310 mila euro a due ex collaboratori per averli licenziati senza giusta causa nel 2025. La sentenza del Tribunale di Milano, depositata nel marzo scorso, riconosce il risarcimento per i mancati guadagni e le spese legali. Salis ha già presentato ricorso in appello. La condanna è stata emessa dopo che i legali dei collaboratori non sono riusciti a notificare gli atti alla residenza ufficiale dell'eurodeputata.

stories.title: 'Would Beckham do it?' - Fury stands by Thailand fight
stories.summary: Tyson Fury will face Mariusz Wach in a non-broadcast fight in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, with a win potentially setting up a long-awaited showdown with Anthony Joshua. Fury, 37, has won 35 pro fights and will face 46-year-old Pole Mariusz Wach. The fight will not be broadcast live, but a win will set up a potential showdown with fellow Briton Anthony Joshua, who faces Kristian Prenga in Saudi Arabia on Saturday. Fury's decision to fight in Thailand has sparked debate about the priorities of modern-day sports stars.

stories.title: NEW SIGNING INTERVIEW: Filip Bilbija
stories.summary: Derby County has signed Filip Bilbija, a 26-year-old German attacking midfielder, on a three-year free transfer deal after his contract with SC Paderborn expired. Bilbija scored 17 goals in all competitions last season and made 89 Bundesliga 2 appearances for Paderborn. He joins the Rams as their third summer signing and has already linked up with his new teammates in Austria. Bilbija could potentially feature in the friendly against Freiburg on Friday.

stories.title: Preview: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam - prediction, team news, lineups
stories.summary: Bodo/Glimt hosts HamKam at the Aspmyra Stadion on Wednesday, with the hosts sitting third in the Eliteserien, just two points behind leaders Tromso. Bodo/Glimt has gone unbeaten in their last six league matches, scoring 31 goals and conceding 11. HamKam, meanwhile, is without a win in their last three games, including a 4-1 loss to Tromso on Saturday. The match was originally scheduled for March but was postponed due to Bodo/Glimt's Champions League run.

stories.title: L'Inter punta Romero! Costa tanto ma è in rotta con il Tottenham. E c'è l'amico Lautaro che...
stories.summary: Italian football club Inter is targeting Tottenham's Cristian Romero to strengthen its defense. The 29-year-old Argentine center back has six World Cup appearances, including the final, and has expressed interest in joining Inter. Romero last played against Inter in 2021, while with Atalanta. Inter is looking to bolster its defense ahead of the new Serie A season.

stories.title: Levski Sofia vs Universitatea Craiova Predictions, Picks & Odds – 22/07/2026
stories.summary: Levski Sofia hosts Universitatea Craiova in the UEFA Champions League 2nd Qualifying Round at the Vivacom Arena in Sofia on July 22, 2026. Levski Sofia arrives with a ten-game unbeaten streak, conceding just 0.6 goals per match. Universitatea Craiova is also unbeaten in 15 consecutive matches, posing a serious threat. The match is expected to be closely contested.

stories.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
stories.summary: Matthis Abline, a 23-year-old attacker, is close to joining AS Monaco in a record transfer deal worth around 30 million euros, surpassing the previous record of 20 million euros for Nathan Zézé's transfer to Neom in 2025. Abline has already agreed to join Monaco, and the deal is expected to be finalized soon. This transfer would be a significant departure for FC Nantes, who are looking to preserve their player's value.

stories.title: Wie verstärkt sich der BVB nach den Abgängen von Brandt und Adeyemi?
stories.summary: Borussia Dortmund is strengthening its team after the departures of Julian Brandt and Karim Adeyemi. The club has already signed three new players, including Justin Lerma, Kaua Prates, and Jodao Gadou, and is expected to announce another signing soon. A deal worth 22 million euros for Karim Adeyemi is set to be officially confirmed, with the transfer fee to be paid in installments. The team's preparation for the upcoming season is underway, with the Bundesliga resuming after a break.

stories.title: L'estate bollente di Bastoni: il mercato, le parole dell'agente, l'Inter che se lo tiene stretto
stories.summary: Italian defender Alessandro Bastoni remains a priority for Inter Milan, despite interest from Al-Hilal, led by coach Simone Inzaghi. The Saudi club has expressed interest in Bastoni, but no official offer has been made, and Inter is not considering selling him. Bastoni's current contract is set to expire, but he has expressed a desire to renew it. The situation remains unchanged, with Inter maintaining control over the player's future.

stories.title: Los cazas F-16 ucranianos pasan a la ofensiva: EEUU confirma el derribo de un Su-35 ruso
stories.summary: A Ukrainian F-16 fighter jet has downed a Russian Su-35S fighter jet in a dogfight, marking the first time a Ukrainian aircraft has achieved an air-to-air victory. The incident occurred on July 8, with the US confirming the F-16's role in the destruction. This development marks a shift in the war, with Western fighter jets now engaging in offensive air superiority missions against Russian aircraft. The US general has also praised Ukraine's progress in air-to-air combat and defense capabilities.

stories.title: Schipper Joure: "We hebben echt enorm staan schreeuwen naar het publiek"
stories.summary: A skûtsje, a traditional Dutch sailing vessel, crashed into a crowd of spectators during a competition in Friesland, resulting in minor damage and no reported injuries. The incident occurred when two skûtsjes collided, causing one to veer off course and strike a dock where people were standing. The skûtsje's crew is calling for rule changes to prevent such incidents in the future. An investigation into the cause of the collision is underway.

stories.title: INFO OUEST-FRANCE. À Rennes, une femme de 80 ans violée dans son hall d’immeuble par un jeune de 16 ans
stories.summary: A 16-year-old boy has been arrested in Rennes, France, on suspicion of raping an 80-year-old woman in her apartment building on July 4. The suspect was identified through DNA analysis and was found in possession of cocaine. He is a minor non-accompanied and homeless, and has been placed in custody, set to appear before the Rennes prosecutor on Thursday.

stories.title: Cyril Hanouna et Shana Loustau en couple ? "Il n'y a plus de doute", cette vidéo de leur prestigieux dîner fait bondir
stories.summary: French TV host Cyril Hanouna and journalist Shana Loustau have sparked speculation about their relationship after appearing together at a charity gala in Saint-Tropez. The event, organized by Cathy Guetta, raised funds for the "Cathy Guetta for Sickle Cell" association. Hanouna and Loustau were seen sharing a moment of complicity, but neither has confirmed being in a couple. The gala featured a high-end auction with luxury items, including a stay at the Setai Miami Beach and a Céline Dion concert experience.

stories.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
stories.summary: Max Wöber has left Leeds United after the club and player agreed to mutually terminate his contract. The Austrian defender, who joined Leeds in 2023, made 19 appearances in his debut season and 8 league appearances in the 2024/25 campaign before joining Werder Bremen on loan. Wöber is expected to join Bundesliga side Schalke on a free transfer. He will now return to Germany, where he has completed the first part of his medical.

stories.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
stories.summary: Harry Potter films return to Dutch cinemas for a limited time to celebrate the 25th anniversary of the first film. Pathé will screen the series from August 26 to September 2, culminating in a 24-hour marathon on August 29-30. This is reportedly the last opportunity to see the films in Dutch cinemas. The event will feature a quiz, festival band, and breakfast for the final film.

stories.title: Heathrow switched to single runway operations following British Airways BA547 emergency landing
stories.summary: A British Airways Airbus A320 from Rome declared an emergency over London Heathrow today after a hydraulic failure forced a go-around at 1:09 PM. The airport switched to single-runway operations briefly while the crew assessed the issue. The flight later diverted to Liverpool without further incident. Heathrow has not commented on the duration of the runway restriction.

stories.title: Miljoenenverkoop Cissoko keert drie jaar na vertrek bij NEC terug in Eredivisie
stories.summary: Ibrahim Cissoko returns to the Eredivisie three years after leaving NEC, signing a contract with PEC Zwolle until 2030. The 30-year-old left NEC for Toulouse in 2020, but played only 15 official matches in three years. Cissoko hopes to regain his top form in Zwolle and contribute to the team's goals and assists. He joins fellow left-back Elias Sørensen at PEC Zwolle.

stories.title: West Ham sanction Summerville exit as €70m transfer hijack agreed
stories.summary: West Ham have sanctioned the sale of forward Crysencio Summerville to a Saudi Pro League club for a reported €70m. The Netherlands international, who scored five Premier League goals last season, will undergo a medical ahead of a long-term deal. Roma had submitted multiple bids but Al Hilal’s offer was accepted. West Ham have also retained Jarrod Bowen after he turned down interest from Aston Villa and Everton.

stories.title: Here’s Why Rosalía Is Apologizing to Argentine Fans Following Spain’s World Cup Victory
stories.summary: Rosalía apologized to Argentine fans after reposting a controversial Instagram Story mocking Spain’s World Cup victory over Argentina. The July 19 final in New Jersey sparked backlash when a clip lip-syncing her song "La Perla" suggested celebrating Argentina’s defeat. The apology came on August 22, ahead of her Buenos Aires concert on August 1. She later clarified her support for Argentina.

stories.title: Waterford man, 36, charged over suspected stabbing at Tramore church
stories.summary: A 36-year-old Waterford man has been charged with assault causing harm and possession of a knife following an incident at Holy Cross Church in Tramore, Co Waterford. Niall Cummins appeared in court on suspicion of the attack on a woman in her 50s. The charges were filed after gardaí investigated the alleged stabbing at the church on Summerhill. Further court proceedings are scheduled.

stories.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
stories.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna after being restrained by police during a confrontation in a confused state. An autopsy is underway to determine the cause of death and assess any pre-existing health issues. Authorities say officers acted under protocol while family members cite possible medical factors. Investigators will review the incident as political tensions rise over law enforcement accountability.

stories.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
stories.summary: Liverpool have made an initial approach for Monaco winger Maghnes Akliouche, 24, as PSG also pursue the France international. The Reds checked his availability amid ongoing transfer interest. Monaco has rejected multiple PSG offers so far. A deal could hinge on further negotiations in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Cape Verde hero Vozinha enters in FIFA's Best XI from World Cup, with three World Champions from Spain
stories.summary: Cape Verde’s 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha was named to FIFA’s Best XI for the World Cup, becoming the tournament’s surprise hero after making eight saves against Argentina. Spain led the selections with three players, while France and Argentina each had two. England’s lone representative was not Harry Kane. The final squad includes Messi, Mbappé, and Haaland.

stories.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
stories.summary: The PSOE reaffirmed its support for former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on Wednesday, stating that only the judicial investigation into the Plus Ultra airline rescue can determine the truth of recent allegations. Agriculture Minister Luis Planas linked businessman Julio Martínez’s claims to a defense strategy amid the ongoing case, stressing Zapatero’s presumption of innocence while urging factual explanations. The investigation follows the airline’s pandemic-era rescue, part of a broader EU-backed liquidity fund for struggling companies. A court ruling is expected as the process continues.

stories.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
stories.summary: Prosecutors in Rome sought chat records between Deputy Justice Minister Andrea Delmastro and a mafia-linked restaurateur, but the Senate’s Authorisations Committee blocked the request. Five Star Movement senators occupied government benches for over 30 minutes, chanting for the logs’ release before the sitting was suspended. The centre-right voted to uphold the rejection, citing vague scope and lack of time limits in the prosecutors’ request. The decision halts further action on the matter pending new justification.

stories.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
stories.summary: Two trams collided on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge around 11:10 a.m., leaving 15 injured, including one in serious condition. Emergency crews freed a trapped passenger and treated others on-site or at area hospitals. The crash damaged both trams and closed the bridge to all traffic. Authorities have adjusted injury counts from earlier reports.

stories.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
stories.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family. Funeral services will be private, with no public arrangements.
stories.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

stories.title: Sacha Baron Cohen’s ‘Ali G: Who Iz I?’ Sets October Release From Amazon MGM
stories.summary: Sacha Baron Cohen's "Ali G: Who Iz I?" is set to release in theaters on October 23. The film marks Cohen's feature directorial debut, in which he also stars as the character he originated in the 1990s. Cohen directed the movie secretly, similar to his 2006 "Borat" comedy and its 2020 sequel. The film will be released by Amazon MGM Studios.

stories.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
stories.summary: Natalie Fleet, Labour MP and safeguarding minister, has stepped down from her role citing overwhelming triggering content. She was appointed on May 12 this year, following the resignation of Jess Phillips. Fleet, who was groomed and raped as a teenager, described her departure as the "bravest thing I've ever done" and offered support to her successor. She emphasized the importance of a competent minister for the role, which deals with the worst of humanity.

stories.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly developing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely feature 12GB of RAM, an upgrade from the original 8GB. The new model is expected to cost more due to ongoing component shortages. Apple is also planning to release new iMacs with OLED screens. The updated Neo is expected to be released in the coming months.

stories.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
stories.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and his crewmates are concluding a 241-day mission aboard the International Space Station, with a scheduled undocking from the orbiting laboratory at 3:02 a.m. EDT Sunday, July 26. The crew will parachute-land on the steppe of Kazakhstan, southeast of Dzhezkazgan, after completing 3,856 orbits and traveling over 102 million miles. NASA's live return coverage will be streamed through various platforms. The crew will then fly to Karaganda, Kazakhstan, for recovery and eventual return to their respective training bases.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that ultra-processed foods are a primary driver of obesity in the country. The report recommends targeting sugar and refined carbohydrates in food labeling and reformulation, rather than fat and salt. This shift in focus aims to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for promoting healthier options. The change is expected to be implemented in food policy, potentially reducing obesity rates in Britain.

stories.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
stories.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire replied with 118-8, led by Georgia Voll's 52. The Brave's fightback was sparked by 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman and finished by captain Sophie Molineux's 3-14. The Brave will look to build momentum in their campaign.

stories.title: Why are cycling's anti-doping tests being carried out at night?
stories.summary: Cycling's anti-doping tests are being carried out at night for the first time in years, with five teams having riders tested between 11pm and 6am during the 2026 Tour de France. Yellow jersey leader Tadej Pogacar and his main rival Jonas Vingegaard were among those tested, with Vingegaard crashing out of the race the following day. The nocturnal tests are seen as a way to prevent doping, with some in the sport calling it an effective measure. The move is a significant shift in the sport's anti-doping efforts, which have been criticized in the past.

stories.title: Eccles and Salmon given Team Wales honour in Glasgow
stories.summary: Reigning Commonwealth Games champions Rosie Eccles and Daniel Salmon have been chosen as Team Wales' flag and baton bearers for the opening ceremony in Glasgow. Eccles, a 30-year-old boxer from Caldicot, will celebrate her birthday by carrying the flag and leading Wales out at the Hydro on Thursday night. This is her third and final Games appearance, having won silver in 2018 and gold in Birmingham. Eccles will now lead a team of athletes onto the stage, a moment she describes as "unreal" and a "huge honour".

stories.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
stories.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has unveiled a new image of the spiral galaxy Messier 94, also known as NGC 4736, on June 30, 2026. The galaxy features a bright inner ring where new stars are forming, fueled by gas in its unique oval-shaped structure. The image combines X-ray data from Chandra with a visible light image from ground-based telescopes. This new look at Messier 94 is now available to experience through an accompanying sound component.

stories.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
stories.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star alongside Ben Stiller in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody", marking Del Toro's first lead role in a comedy series and a reunion with Stiller. The show follows a disgraced financier accused of massive fraud, navigating prison politics and consequences. Del Toro is a three-time Oscar nominee and a Golden Globe winner. The series is expected to premiere on Apple TV.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants were arrested in August and denied the charges. The case is seen as a significant blow to Russia's influence in Angola.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off on pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has withdrawn his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of legal authority. Mamdani now urges US officials to enforce the International Criminal Court’s arrest warrant, though the US is not an ICC member. Netanyahu faces allegations of war crimes, with the ICC warrant issued amid ongoing conflicts. Trump has publicly stated Netanyahu would not be arrested during any US visit.

stories.title: ‘Days of Thunder 2’: Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise in Paramount Sequel
stories.summary: Tom Cruise is set to star in a sequel to the 1990 film "Days of Thunder," with Jonathan Levine in talks to direct. The film will be produced by Cruise and Jerry Bruckheimer. Paramount is reportedly moving forward with the project. The sequel's script was written by Will Staples.

stories.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?
stories.summary: A youth-led movement angry over the education system and a lack of jobs is posing a rare challenge to a government that has ruled for more than a decade with a firm hand.

stories.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman in France has been formally charged with the April 2024 stabbing death of British-South African tourist Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. Prosecutors confirmed the charge on Wednesday, following a judicial inquiry opened in May 2025. Carter, a mother of four who had lived in the Dordogne village for over a decade, was found near her car with stab wounds. Authorities originally detained the suspect in 2025 before releasing her pending further investigation.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
stories.summary: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

stories.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
stories.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
stories.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 out of 173 tested towers have tested positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to investigate and test cooling towers. Further testing is expected to reveal the full extent of the outbreak.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

offbeat.title: PHOTOS: Spain wins the World Cup by beating Argentina 1-0 in extra time
offbeat.summary: Spain won the 2026 FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time on July 19 in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal as Spain claimed their first World Cup title. The victory follows Spain’s strong performance in the tournament, with the team now favored for the 2027 Women’s World Cup alongside the U.S. Next, attention turns to the upcoming Women’s World Cup in 2027, where the U.S. and Spain are early betting favorites.

offbeat.title: Modeling agent who introduced women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
offbeat.summary: A French modeling agent linked to Jeffrey Epstein was found dead at his home near Paris on Monday. Daniel Siad, 69, had been under investigation for allegedly helping recruit women for Epstein over a decade. French prosecutors opened an inquiry into his death and ordered an autopsy. Investigators had not yet questioned Siad, who denied wrongdoing.

offbeat.title: Will Lionel Messi win the Ballon d'Or, and when will Argentina's World Cup star rejoin Inter Miami?
offbeat.summary: Argentina’s World Cup final loss on July 19, 2026 ended Lionel Messi’s 2026 Ballon d’Or hopes, as the evaluation period closes July 31. FIFA’s 21-day rest rule keeps him off Inter Miami’s next three matches, including games against Chicago, Montreal and Columbus. Messi’s return to club play hinges on Leagues Cup action, with Inter Miami opening August 5 against Atletico de San Luis. The tournament runs through August 12 at home in Miami.

offbeat.title: The video of the Argentina national team in the tunnel that buries the conspiracy theories about the defeat against Spain
offbeat.summary: A newly released tunnel video from Argentina’s World Cup campaign debunks conspiracy theories about their 2026 final defeat to Spain. The three-minute footage shows Messi rallying teammates before the match at MetLife Stadium, emphasizing focus over external noise. The match drew 63 million U.S. viewers, the most for any soccer game in the country’s history. Argentina’s next club commitments begin after a mandatory 21-day World Cup rest period.

offbeat.title: Four teens to appear in court after ‘serious’ Virginia incident
offbeat.summary: Four juveniles will appear in Cavan District Children's Court Thursday at 10:30 a.m. charged with a serious public order incident in Virginia, Co Cavan, Wednesday evening. Three people suffered significant but non-life-threatening injuries, and local waterways were searched after allegations of a weapon. Gardaí confirmed the arrests following an investigation into the Main Street disturbance. Further details are pending as inquiries continue.

offbeat.title: Garnacho becomes third Man Utd flop to join Aston Villa in three years as he completes medical for Chelsea loan
offbeat.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on a season-long loan from Chelsea with a conditional obligation to buy. The 22-year-old Argentine winger completed his medical and is finalizing the deal in Birmingham. The move follows a disappointing first season at Chelsea, where he scored eight goals in 43 appearances. Villa manager Unai Emery has agreed to a four-year contract if the permanent transfer is triggered next summer.

offbeat.title: ResilExec Coaching Launches Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™ -- Completing a Full Career Architecture Platform for Mid-Career Leaders in Healthcare and Enterprise IT
offbeat.summary: ResilExec Coaching launched two new career programs—Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™—expanding its platform for mid-career leaders in healthcare and enterprise IT. The additions complement the existing Purpose-Driven Ascent™ program, addressing external job transitions and post-promotion career stability. Founder Brian Baldari’s 8-phase system targets Associate Directors through VPs navigating layoffs or voluntary moves. The full platform now covers three key career moments: internal promotion, external re-entry, and long-term trajectory maintenance.

offbeat.title: Jasper Philipsen wins stage 17 of Tour de France, Tadej Pogačar protects lead
offbeat.summary: Belgian Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the 2026 Tour de France in Voiron on July 22, securing his 11th career stage victory. Defending champion Tadej Pogačar maintained his overall lead with a 4-minute, 32-second advantage. Philipsen’s victory extends Belgium’s streak of stage wins to three in a row. The race resumes Thursday with a mountainous stage from Voiron to Orcieres-Merlette.

offbeat.title: Was Daniel Siad ever married? Details on family amid key Epstein model scout's mystery death in Paris | Trending
offbeat.summary: French modeling scout Daniel Siad, linked to Jeffrey Epstein’s network, was found dead at his Paris-area home on Monday. The 69-year-old’s death prompted an investigation and autopsy, though authorities have not cited foul play. Siad’s name appeared hundreds of times in recently released Epstein probe files. Authorities continue to review his connections to the case.

offbeat.title: Kyle Kelly and Omari Patrick: Forest Green Rovers sign duo
offbeat.summary: Forest Green Rovers have signed midfielder Kyle Kelly on a six-month loan from Liverpool and forward Omari Patrick on a permanent deal. Kelly, 20, has eight caps for St Kitts and Nevis, while Patrick, 29, joins after scoring seven goals in 41 games for Tranmere last season. The club confirmed both signings ahead of the new campaign. Forest Green aim to strengthen their squad for the upcoming League Two fixtures.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 - Schedule, draw and prize money
offbeat.summary: The 2026 World Matchplay Darts tournament begins on Saturday, July 18 at Winter Gardens in Blackpool, featuring 32 players, including defending champion Luke Littler, the teenage world number one. The event is one of the Professional Darts Corporation's eight major events. The tournament will conclude on Sunday, July 26 with a women's event. The top 16-ranked players have been seeded to compete in the open draw.

offbeat.title: Braunau in Österreich: Hitlers Geburtshaus wird zu Polizeistation
offbeat.summary: Hitler's birthplace in Braunau, Austria, is being converted into a police station, a move aimed at neutralizing the site and deterring far-right groups. The building, previously a school and home to a disability support organization, has been vacant since 2017. The police station is expected to serve as a deterrent and help end discussions over the site's future use. The move comes as the city hopes to prevent the building from becoming a pilgrimage site for extremist groups.

offbeat.title: Gearóid Hegarty enjoying special days alongside his best friends
offbeat.summary: Limerick's Gearóid Hegarty and his teammates are celebrating their sixth All-Ireland senior hurling title after defeating Galway in the final. Hegarty's bond with his teammates, including best friends David Reidy and Seán Finn, has been key to their success. The group will soon disperse, with Hegarty set to get married to Niamh McCarthy in Portugal, and many of the players will not reconvene until mid-winter. Hegarty's selfless play, such as laying off a puckout to Reidy for a point, has been a hallmark of their championship run.

offbeat.title: USA: New Jersey registrierte versehentlich mehr als 6500 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler
offbeat.summary: In New Jersey wurden vor der Präsidentschaftswahl 2024 etwa 6600 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler registriert, aufgrund eines ernsten Softwarefehlers. Weniger als 400 von ihnen haben tatsächlich gewählt. Die demokratische Gouverneurin Mikie Sherrill hat sich über die verantwortungslosen Versäumnisse und den Mangel an Transparenz geäußert, die diese Registrierungen ermöglicht haben. Die Registrierungen fanden zwischen Juni 2023 und Juni 2024 statt.

offbeat.title: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam Prediction, Betting Tips, Lineups & Odds | 22 Jul 2026
offbeat.summary: Bodo/Glimt hosts HamKam at Aspmyra Stadion on July 22, with the home team seeking to extend their unbeaten run in the Eliteserien to seven matches. Bodo/Glimt has scored 31 goals this season, the most in the league, while HamKam has lost its last three games. The two teams have a history of close matches, with Bodo/Glimt winning their last meeting 3-1. Bodo/Glimt is looking to maintain its strong home form, having lost only one game at home this season.

offbeat.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
offbeat.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a man hiding behind a wall in a Wexford holiday home they had booked through Booking.com. The incident occurred last weekend when Therese and her family returned from a family party to find the landlord hiding behind a wall. The property has been suspended by Booking.com while an investigation is ongoing. The company has launched an investigation into the incident.

offbeat.title: Paul Nardi, un renfort d’expérience pour l’AJA
offbeat.summary: Paul Nardi, 32 ans, rejoint l’AJ Auxerre pour deux saisons jusqu’en juin 2028. Le gardien, ancien international Espoirs français, arrive en provenance des Queens Park Rangers où il a joué 63 matchs. Formé à l’AS Nancy-Lorraine, il a évolué en Ligue 1, Ligue 2 et à l’étranger, remportant notamment la Ligue 2 avec le FC Lorient en 2019. Il portera le numéro 1 et apportera son expérience à l’équipe pour la saison à venir.

offbeat.title: Text of new Gordie Howe bridge deal seems to contradict Carney
offbeat.summary: The Gordie Howe International Bridge near Windsor, Ontario, is set to open to traffic on July 27, despite recent trade tensions between Canada and the US. The bridge's opening ceremony, initially planned as a joint event, has been cancelled due to US President Donald Trump's threat to raise tariffs on Canadian imports. The deal to open the bridge includes a proposed agreement in principle, where Canada will split half of the bridge's net revenue with an economic development fund for the first 15 years. The bridge's opening will proceed as planned, with a Canada-only event on July 24.

offbeat.title: World Cup 2030 (very early) power rankings: who will challenge Spain at top?
offbeat.summary: Spain will enter the 2030 World Cup as favorites to defend their title, having won the 2026 tournament. The 2030 World Cup will be co-hosted by Spain, Portugal, and Morocco, with the first three matches set to take place in Uruguay, Argentina, and Paraguay. A total of 48 teams will participate in the tournament, up from 32 in previous years. The host nations are still deciding on the venue for the final match.

offbeat.title: Manuel Barragán vuelve a actuar después de casi perder la vida: "No hay nada que hacer"
offbeat.summary: Spanish comedian Manuel Barragán has made a remarkable recovery after nearly losing his life in a car accident, spending 40 days in the hospital, 17 of them in intensive care. The accident was caused by a heart condition that weakened his heart and led to a severe infarction. Barragán's family says his recovery was a miracle, and he has since returned to the hospital to thank the medical staff who saved his life.

offbeat.title: Wrexham to appoint Newcastle's Nickson as sporting director
offbeat.summary: Wrexham is set to appoint Steve Nickson as its first-ever sporting director on September 2, bringing 15 years of experience from Newcastle United. Nickson will work closely with manager Phil Parkinson to bolster the club's hopes of reaching the Premier League. This move follows Wrexham's highest league finish of seventh in the Championship and represents a significant shift in the club's transfer market operations. The appointment is seen as a coup, with West Ham United and Birmingham City also interested in securing Nickson's services.

offbeat.title: En direct Football. Le quatrième but du FC Metz face au FC Sochaux-Montbéliard : suivez le sixième match de préparation des Grenats en direct
offbeat.summary: FC Metz defeated FC Sochaux-Montbéliard 4-1 in a pre-season friendly on July 22, 2026, at Biesheim Stadium in Haut-Rhin. Pape Moussa Fall scored a hat-trick, with Jessy Deminguet and Mathis Clairicia adding the other goals. Metz dominated early, leading 3-0 by halftime before Sochaux pulled one back late. The result caps a strong start to Metz’s summer preparation ahead of the 2026-27 Ligue 2 campaign.

offbeat.title: Far Away, le anticipazioni delle puntate dal 20 al 24 luglio
offbeat.summary: La serie tv turca "Far Away" continua con nuove puntate dal 20 al 24 luglio. La storia segue le vicende di Alya e Cihan, che si avvicinano nonostante le opposizioni di Mine. La puntata del 20 luglio vedrà Alya e Cihan chiacchierare al bar dell'ospedale, mentre la puntata del 22 luglio sarà segnata dalla fuga di Kaya e Zerrin. La tensione tra le due famiglie sale in tutta fretta e Cihan cercherà di rintracciare Kaya prima che ci riescano i Nalhanoglu. Le puntate saranno in onda su Canale 5 e in streaming su Mediaset Infinity.

offbeat.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
offbeat.summary: Spanish influencer Lucía Pombo and pilot Álvaro López-Huertas have welcomed their first child, a daughter named Lola Belén, born on July 21. The couple, who met as children through their parents' friendship, had been expecting the arrival of their baby for several weeks. With López-Huertas by her side, Pombo has expressed her excitement and relief at finally giving birth after a long and challenging pregnancy.

offbeat.title: Bohemians seek inspiration on the last European night at the old Dalymount Park
offbeat.summary: Bohemians host Ballkani at Dalymount Park in a Uefa Conference League qualifying round two first-leg match, with the Irish side looking to build on their recent consistency and home advantage after a 1-0 lead was taken to Tallaght in the first round. Bohemians are second in the League of Ireland, seven points behind Shamrock Rovers, and will be seeking a strong performance from their lone striker, Colm Whelan, who has scored nine goals this season. The match takes place on Thursday at 6pm. A win for Bohemians would set them up well for the return game in Kosovo next week.

offbeat.title: Hospital apologises after man's death in Galway
offbeat.summary: A 26-year-old Portuguese man, Henrique Coelho, died two days after being discharged from Galway University Hospital's emergency department in January 2024. The hospital has apologised for deficiencies in his care, which led to his death. His family has been awarded €35,000 in damages, with his mother receiving €20,000 and his ex-wife €15,000. The family had claimed negligence and breach of duty by the hospital.

offbeat.title: Asyl-Beratungsmobil fährt seit Februar durch den Landkreis
offbeat.summary: A mobile counseling unit has been operating in the Weilheim-Schongau district since February, providing on-site assistance to asylum seekers. The unit, which is a converted van, has already counseled around 70 people in 14 sessions. The unit is staffed by integration and refugee advisors from the district office, whose positions are also funded by the state of Bavaria. The unit's deployment plan is available on the district office's website.

offbeat.title: 10 clubs paid the most by FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup
offbeat.summary: Manchester City is set to receive an estimated $4.4 million from FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup, the largest payout among 10 clubs. The English champions topped the list compiled by The Athletic, ahead of Barcelona and Bayern Munich. FIFA will distribute $355 million to clubs that released players for the tournament, a 70% increase from previous tournaments. The payments will be based on the number of days players spent with their national teams.

offbeat.title: Tottenham vs. MK Dons: how to watch preseason friendly
offbeat.summary: Tottenham Hotspur is hosting MK Dons in a preseason friendly match at the Hotspur Way training center, marking the club's return to action after the World Cup. The match is a closed-door friendly, focusing on fitness and learning tactics rather than winning. The game is available to watch exclusively on SpursPLAY, Tottenham's in-house subscription streaming package. The match is part of Tottenham's preparations for the upcoming season.

offbeat.title: Avs, Ilaria Salis condannata per aver licenziato due collaboratori senza giusta causa
offbeat.summary: Ilaria Salis, eurodeputata di Avs, è stata condannata a risarcire oltre 310 mila euro a due ex collaboratori per averli licenziati senza giusta causa nel 2025. La sentenza del Tribunale di Milano, depositata nel marzo scorso, riconosce il risarcimento per i mancati guadagni e le spese legali. Salis ha già presentato ricorso in appello. La condanna è stata emessa dopo che i legali dei collaboratori non sono riusciti a notificare gli atti alla residenza ufficiale dell'eurodeputata.

offbeat.title: 'Would Beckham do it?' - Fury stands by Thailand fight
offbeat.summary: Tyson Fury will face Mariusz Wach in a non-broadcast fight in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, with a win potentially setting up a long-awaited showdown with Anthony Joshua. Fury, 37, has won 35 pro fights and will face 46-year-old Pole Mariusz Wach. The fight will not be broadcast live, but a win will set up a potential showdown with fellow Briton Anthony Joshua, who faces Kristian Prenga in Saudi Arabia on Saturday. Fury's decision to fight in Thailand has sparked debate about the priorities of modern-day sports stars.

offbeat.title: NEW SIGNING INTERVIEW: Filip Bilbija
offbeat.summary: Derby County has signed Filip Bilbija, a 26-year-old German attacking midfielder, on a three-year free transfer deal after his contract with SC Paderborn expired. Bilbija scored 17 goals in all competitions last season and made 89 Bundesliga 2 appearances for Paderborn. He joins the Rams as their third summer signing and has already linked up with his new teammates in Austria. Bilbija could potentially feature in the friendly against Freiburg on Friday.

offbeat.title: Preview: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam - prediction, team news, lineups
offbeat.summary: Bodo/Glimt hosts HamKam at the Aspmyra Stadion on Wednesday, with the hosts sitting third in the Eliteserien, just two points behind leaders Tromso. Bodo/Glimt has gone unbeaten in their last six league matches, scoring 31 goals and conceding 11. HamKam, meanwhile, is without a win in their last three games, including a 4-1 loss to Tromso on Saturday. The match was originally scheduled for March but was postponed due to Bodo/Glimt's Champions League run.

offbeat.title: L'Inter punta Romero! Costa tanto ma è in rotta con il Tottenham. E c'è l'amico Lautaro che...
offbeat.summary: Italian football club Inter is targeting Tottenham's Cristian Romero to strengthen its defense. The 29-year-old Argentine center back has six World Cup appearances, including the final, and has expressed interest in joining Inter. Romero last played against Inter in 2021, while with Atalanta. Inter is looking to bolster its defense ahead of the new Serie A season.

offbeat.title: Levski Sofia vs Universitatea Craiova Predictions, Picks & Odds – 22/07/2026
offbeat.summary: Levski Sofia hosts Universitatea Craiova in the UEFA Champions League 2nd Qualifying Round at the Vivacom Arena in Sofia on July 22, 2026. Levski Sofia arrives with a ten-game unbeaten streak, conceding just 0.6 goals per match. Universitatea Craiova is also unbeaten in 15 consecutive matches, posing a serious threat. The match is expected to be closely contested.

offbeat.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
offbeat.summary: Matthis Abline, a 23-year-old attacker, is close to joining AS Monaco in a record transfer deal worth around 30 million euros, surpassing the previous record of 20 million euros for Nathan Zézé's transfer to Neom in 2025. Abline has already agreed to join Monaco, and the deal is expected to be finalized soon. This transfer would be a significant departure for FC Nantes, who are looking to preserve their player's value.

offbeat.title: Wie verstärkt sich der BVB nach den Abgängen von Brandt und Adeyemi?
offbeat.summary: Borussia Dortmund is strengthening its team after the departures of Julian Brandt and Karim Adeyemi. The club has already signed three new players, including Justin Lerma, Kaua Prates, and Jodao Gadou, and is expected to announce another signing soon. A deal worth 22 million euros for Karim Adeyemi is set to be officially confirmed, with the transfer fee to be paid in installments. The team's preparation for the upcoming season is underway, with the Bundesliga resuming after a break.

offbeat.title: L'estate bollente di Bastoni: il mercato, le parole dell'agente, l'Inter che se lo tiene stretto
offbeat.summary: Italian defender Alessandro Bastoni remains a priority for Inter Milan, despite interest from Al-Hilal, led by coach Simone Inzaghi. The Saudi club has expressed interest in Bastoni, but no official offer has been made, and Inter is not considering selling him. Bastoni's current contract is set to expire, but he has expressed a desire to renew it. The situation remains unchanged, with Inter maintaining control over the player's future.

offbeat.title: Los cazas F-16 ucranianos pasan a la ofensiva: EEUU confirma el derribo de un Su-35 ruso
offbeat.summary: A Ukrainian F-16 fighter jet has downed a Russian Su-35S fighter jet in a dogfight, marking the first time a Ukrainian aircraft has achieved an air-to-air victory. The incident occurred on July 8, with the US confirming the F-16's role in the destruction. This development marks a shift in the war, with Western fighter jets now engaging in offensive air superiority missions against Russian aircraft. The US general has also praised Ukraine's progress in air-to-air combat and defense capabilities.

offbeat.title: Schipper Joure: "We hebben echt enorm staan schreeuwen naar het publiek"
offbeat.summary: A skûtsje, a traditional Dutch sailing vessel, crashed into a crowd of spectators during a competition in Friesland, resulting in minor damage and no reported injuries. The incident occurred when two skûtsjes collided, causing one to veer off course and strike a dock where people were standing. The skûtsje's crew is calling for rule changes to prevent such incidents in the future. An investigation into the cause of the collision is underway.

offbeat.title: INFO OUEST-FRANCE. À Rennes, une femme de 80 ans violée dans son hall d’immeuble par un jeune de 16 ans
offbeat.summary: A 16-year-old boy has been arrested in Rennes, France, on suspicion of raping an 80-year-old woman in her apartment building on July 4. The suspect was identified through DNA analysis and was found in possession of cocaine. He is a minor non-accompanied and homeless, and has been placed in custody, set to appear before the Rennes prosecutor on Thursday.

offbeat.title: Cyril Hanouna et Shana Loustau en couple ? "Il n'y a plus de doute", cette vidéo de leur prestigieux dîner fait bondir
offbeat.summary: French TV host Cyril Hanouna and journalist Shana Loustau have sparked speculation about their relationship after appearing together at a charity gala in Saint-Tropez. The event, organized by Cathy Guetta, raised funds for the "Cathy Guetta for Sickle Cell" association. Hanouna and Loustau were seen sharing a moment of complicity, but neither has confirmed being in a couple. The gala featured a high-end auction with luxury items, including a stay at the Setai Miami Beach and a Céline Dion concert experience.

offbeat.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
offbeat.summary: Max Wöber has left Leeds United after the club and player agreed to mutually terminate his contract. The Austrian defender, who joined Leeds in 2023, made 19 appearances in his debut season and 8 league appearances in the 2024/25 campaign before joining Werder Bremen on loan. Wöber is expected to join Bundesliga side Schalke on a free transfer. He will now return to Germany, where he has completed the first part of his medical.

offbeat.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
offbeat.summary: Harry Potter films return to Dutch cinemas for a limited time to celebrate the 25th anniversary of the first film. Pathé will screen the series from August 26 to September 2, culminating in a 24-hour marathon on August 29-30. This is reportedly the last opportunity to see the films in Dutch cinemas. The event will feature a quiz, festival band, and breakfast for the final film.

offbeat.title: Heathrow switched to single runway operations following British Airways BA547 emergency landing
offbeat.summary: A British Airways Airbus A320 from Rome declared an emergency over London Heathrow today after a hydraulic failure forced a go-around at 1:09 PM. The airport switched to single-runway operations briefly while the crew assessed the issue. The flight later diverted to Liverpool without further incident. Heathrow has not commented on the duration of the runway restriction.

offbeat.title: Miljoenenverkoop Cissoko keert drie jaar na vertrek bij NEC terug in Eredivisie
offbeat.summary: Ibrahim Cissoko returns to the Eredivisie three years after leaving NEC, signing a contract with PEC Zwolle until 2030. The 30-year-old left NEC for Toulouse in 2020, but played only 15 official matches in three years. Cissoko hopes to regain his top form in Zwolle and contribute to the team's goals and assists. He joins fellow left-back Elias Sørensen at PEC Zwolle.

offbeat.title: West Ham sanction Summerville exit as €70m transfer hijack agreed
offbeat.summary: West Ham have sanctioned the sale of forward Crysencio Summerville to a Saudi Pro League club for a reported €70m. The Netherlands international, who scored five Premier League goals last season, will undergo a medical ahead of a long-term deal. Roma had submitted multiple bids but Al Hilal’s offer was accepted. West Ham have also retained Jarrod Bowen after he turned down interest from Aston Villa and Everton.

offbeat.title: Here’s Why Rosalía Is Apologizing to Argentine Fans Following Spain’s World Cup Victory
offbeat.summary: Rosalía apologized to Argentine fans after reposting a controversial Instagram Story mocking Spain’s World Cup victory over Argentina. The July 19 final in New Jersey sparked backlash when a clip lip-syncing her song "La Perla" suggested celebrating Argentina’s defeat. The apology came on August 22, ahead of her Buenos Aires concert on August 1. She later clarified her support for Argentina.

offbeat.title: Waterford man, 36, charged over suspected stabbing at Tramore church
offbeat.summary: A 36-year-old Waterford man has been charged with assault causing harm and possession of a knife following an incident at Holy Cross Church in Tramore, Co Waterford. Niall Cummins appeared in court on suspicion of the attack on a woman in her 50s. The charges were filed after gardaí investigated the alleged stabbing at the church on Summerhill. Further court proceedings are scheduled.

offbeat.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
offbeat.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna after being restrained by police during a confrontation in a confused state. An autopsy is underway to determine the cause of death and assess any pre-existing health issues. Authorities say officers acted under protocol while family members cite possible medical factors. Investigators will review the incident as political tensions rise over law enforcement accountability.

offbeat.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
offbeat.summary: Liverpool have made an initial approach for Monaco winger Maghnes Akliouche, 24, as PSG also pursue the France international. The Reds checked his availability amid ongoing transfer interest. Monaco has rejected multiple PSG offers so far. A deal could hinge on further negotiations in the coming weeks.

offbeat.title: Cape Verde hero Vozinha enters in FIFA's Best XI from World Cup, with three World Champions from Spain
offbeat.summary: Cape Verde’s 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha was named to FIFA’s Best XI for the World Cup, becoming the tournament’s surprise hero after making eight saves against Argentina. Spain led the selections with three players, while France and Argentina each had two. England’s lone representative was not Harry Kane. The final squad includes Messi, Mbappé, and Haaland.

offbeat.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
offbeat.summary: The PSOE reaffirmed its support for former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on Wednesday, stating that only the judicial investigation into the Plus Ultra airline rescue can determine the truth of recent allegations. Agriculture Minister Luis Planas linked businessman Julio Martínez’s claims to a defense strategy amid the ongoing case, stressing Zapatero’s presumption of innocence while urging factual explanations. The investigation follows the airline’s pandemic-era rescue, part of a broader EU-backed liquidity fund for struggling companies. A court ruling is expected as the process continues.

offbeat.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
offbeat.summary: Prosecutors in Rome sought chat records between Deputy Justice Minister Andrea Delmastro and a mafia-linked restaurateur, but the Senate’s Authorisations Committee blocked the request. Five Star Movement senators occupied government benches for over 30 minutes, chanting for the logs’ release before the sitting was suspended. The centre-right voted to uphold the rejection, citing vague scope and lack of time limits in the prosecutors’ request. The decision halts further action on the matter pending new justification.

offbeat.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
offbeat.summary: Two trams collided on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge around 11:10 a.m., leaving 15 injured, including one in serious condition. Emergency crews freed a trapped passenger and treated others on-site or at area hospitals. The crash damaged both trams and closed the bridge to all traffic. Authorities have adjusted injury counts from earlier reports.

offbeat.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
offbeat.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family. Funeral services will be private, with no public arrangements.
offbeat.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

offbeat.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
offbeat.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants were arrested in August and denied the charges. The case is seen as a significant blow to Russia's influence in Angola.

offbeat.title: Mamdani backs off on pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
offbeat.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has withdrawn his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing lack of legal authority. Mamdani now urges US officials to enforce the International Criminal Court’s arrest warrant, though the US is not an ICC member. Netanyahu faces allegations of war crimes, with the ICC warrant issued amid ongoing conflicts. Trump has publicly stated Netanyahu would not be arrested during any US visit.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman in France has been formally charged with the April 2024 stabbing death of British-South African tourist Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. Prosecutors confirmed the charge on Wednesday, following a judicial inquiry opened in May 2025. Carter, a mother of four who had lived in the Dordogne village for over a decade, was found near her car with stab wounds. Authorities originally detained the suspect in 2025 before releasing her pending further investigation.

entertainment.title: Sacha Baron Cohen’s ‘Ali G: Who Iz I?’ Sets October Release From Amazon MGM
entertainment.summary: Sacha Baron Cohen's "Ali G: Who Iz I?" is set to release in theaters on October 23. The film marks Cohen's feature directorial debut, in which he also stars as the character he originated in the 1990s. Cohen directed the movie secretly, similar to his 2006 "Borat" comedy and its 2020 sequel. The film will be released by Amazon MGM Studios.

entertainment.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
entertainment.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star alongside Ben Stiller in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody", marking Del Toro's first lead role in a comedy series and a reunion with Stiller. The show follows a disgraced financier accused of massive fraud, navigating prison politics and consequences. Del Toro is a three-time Oscar nominee and a Golden Globe winner. The series is expected to premiere on Apple TV.

entertainment.title: ‘Days of Thunder 2’: Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise in Paramount Sequel
entertainment.summary: Tom Cruise is set to star in a sequel to the 1990 film "Days of Thunder," with Jonathan Levine in talks to direct. The film will be produced by Cruise and Jerry Bruckheimer. Paramount is reportedly moving forward with the project. The sequel's script was written by Will Staples.

politics.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
politics.summary: Natalie Fleet, Labour MP and safeguarding minister, has stepped down from her role citing overwhelming triggering content. She was appointed on May 12 this year, following the resignation of Jess Phillips. Fleet, who was groomed and raped as a teenager, described her departure as the "bravest thing I've ever done" and offered support to her successor. She emphasized the importance of a competent minister for the role, which deals with the worst of humanity.

politics.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
politics.summary: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

politics.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
politics.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

tech.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
tech.summary: Apple is reportedly developing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely feature 12GB of RAM, an upgrade from the original 8GB. The new model is expected to cost more due to ongoing component shortages. Apple is also planning to release new iMacs with OLED screens. The updated Neo is expected to be released in the coming months.

tech.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
tech.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

tech.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
tech.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

facts.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
facts.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and his crewmates are concluding a 241-day mission aboard the International Space Station, with a scheduled undocking from the orbiting laboratory at 3:02 a.m. EDT Sunday, July 26. The crew will parachute-land on the steppe of Kazakhstan, southeast of Dzhezkazgan, after completing 3,856 orbits and traveling over 102 million miles. NASA's live return coverage will be streamed through various platforms. The crew will then fly to Karaganda, Kazakhstan, for recovery and eventual return to their respective training bases.

facts.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
facts.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has unveiled a new image of the spiral galaxy Messier 94, also known as NGC 4736, on June 30, 2026. The galaxy features a bright inner ring where new stars are forming, fueled by gas in its unique oval-shaped structure. The image combines X-ray data from Chandra with a visible light image from ground-based telescopes. This new look at Messier 94 is now available to experience through an accompanying sound component.

facts.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
