facts.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report has found that ultra-processed foods are a primary driver of obesity in the country. The report recommends targeting sugar and refined carbohydrates in food labeling and reformulation, rather than fat and salt. This shift in focus aims to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for promoting healthier options. The change is expected to be implemented in food policy, potentially reducing obesity rates in Britain.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 out of 173 tested towers have tested positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to investigate and test cooling towers. Further testing is expected to reveal the full extent of the outbreak.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

sports.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
sports.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire replied with 118-8, led by Georgia Voll's 52. The Brave's fightback was sparked by 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman and finished by captain Sophie Molineux's 3-14. The Brave will look to build momentum in their campaign.

sports.title: Why are cycling's anti-doping tests being carried out at night?
sports.summary: Cycling's anti-doping tests are being carried out at night for the first time in years, with five teams having riders tested between 11pm and 6am during the 2026 Tour de France. Yellow jersey leader Tadej Pogacar and his main rival Jonas Vingegaard were among those tested, with Vingegaard crashing out of the race the following day. The nocturnal tests are seen as a way to prevent doping, with some in the sport calling it an effective measure. The move is a significant shift in the sport's anti-doping efforts, which have been criticized in the past.

sports.title: Eccles and Salmon given Team Wales honour in Glasgow
sports.summary: Reigning Commonwealth Games champions Rosie Eccles and Daniel Salmon have been chosen as Team Wales' flag and baton bearers for the opening ceremony in Glasgow. Eccles, a 30-year-old boxer from Caldicot, will celebrate her birthday by carrying the flag and leading Wales out at the Hydro on Thursday night. This is her third and final Games appearance, having won silver in 2018 and gold in Birmingham. Eccles will now lead a team of athletes onto the stage, a moment she describes as "unreal" and a "huge honour".

breaking.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?
breaking.summary: A youth-led movement angry over the education system and a lack of jobs is posing a rare challenge to a government that has ruled for more than a decade with a firm hand.

breaking.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
breaking.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

breaking.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
breaking.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

crisis.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
crisis.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.
